chief_complaint: A 25-year-old man presented with five days of high-grade fever, confusion, agitation, productive cough, and dyspnea.
history: A 25-year-old man with a history of type 1 diabetes mellitus (HbA(1)c 10.4%) presented with high-grade fever, confusion, agitation, productive cough, and dyspnea. He was managed for diabetic ketoacidosis and pneumonia with insulin infusion, fluid resuscitation, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and mechanical ventilation. Despite correction of acidosis, fever persisted, and cultures remained negative.
exam: High-grade fever, confusion, agitation, productive cough, and dyspnea. Bilateral lower-lobe pneumonia on chest CT, and mild cerebral edema on CT brain. Hypotension, tachycardia, and hypoxemia.
labs: High-anion-gap metabolic acidosis (pH 7.15, HCO(3)(-) 10 mmol/L), hyperglycemia (427 mg/dL), leucocytosis (23 x 10(3)/microL), hyperferritinemia (2,000 ng/mL), hypertriglyceridemia (337mg/dl-3.805 mmol/l), bicytopenia (Hb 8.5 g/dL; platelets 92 x 10(3)/microL), and an HScore of 198.
correct_diagnosis: Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) with secondary complications of diabetic ketoacidosis and pulmonary embolism
differentials: Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) with secondary complications of diabetic ketoacidosis and pulmonary embolism
Diabetic ketoacidosis with secondary complications of pneumonia and pulmonary embolism
Pulmonary embolism with secondary complications of diabetic ketoacidosis and HLH
Sepsis with secondary complications of pneumonia, diabetic ketoacidosis, and HLH
Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) with secondary complications of pneumonia and HLH
